title: Porsche flat-six engine

infobox.value: 12-valve or 24-valve, [SOHC](/wiki/SOHC "SOHC")/[DOHC](/wiki/DOHC "DOHC"), two-valves per cylinder or four-valves per cylinder[[6]](#cite_note-caranddriver.com-6)

[Mechanical fuel injection](/wiki/Mechanical_fuel_injection "Mechanical fuel injection")  [Carburetor](/wiki/Carburetor "Carburetor")  [Direct fuel injection](/wiki/Gasoline_direct_injection "Gasoline direct injection")

series is a line of mechanically similar [flat-six](/wiki/Flat-six_engine "Flat-six engine") [boxer engines](/wiki/Boxer_engine "Boxer engine"), produced by [Porsche](/wiki/Porsche "Porsche") for over 60 consecutive years, since introduced in the 1963[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) [rear-engine](/wiki/Rear-engine,_rear-wheel-drive_layout "Rear-engine, rear-wheel-drive layout") [Porsche 911](/wiki/Porsche_911_(classic) "Porsche 911 (classic)"), and as [mid-rear mounted](/wiki/Rear_mid-engine,_rear-wheel-drive_layout "Rear mid-engine, rear-wheel-drive layout") racing engine in the 1966 *[Porsche 906](/wiki/Porsche_906 "Porsche 906") Carrera 6*, each with 2000cc.

["Porsche's new 4.0-litre flat-six has "a lot of room" for more power"](https://www.whichcar.com.au/news/porsches-new-40-litre-flat-six-has-a-lot-of-room-for-more-power). 22 July 2019. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20211117091743/https://www.whichcar.com.au/news/porsches-new-40-litre-flat-six-has-a-lot-of-room-for-more-power) from the original on 17 November 2021. Retrieved 17 November 2021.

["How Porsche's brilliant air-cooled flat-six engine thrived for three decades"](https://www.hagerty.com/media/hagerty-magazine/porsches-air-cooled-flat-six-engine-thrived/). 3 October 2019. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20211019000319/https://www.hagerty.com/media/hagerty-magazine/porsches-air-cooled-flat-six-engine-thrived/) from the original on 19 October 2021. Retrieved 19 October 2021.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Reciprocating internal combustion engine

| [Porsche](/wiki/Porsche "Porsche") flat-6 engine | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Overview | |
| [Manufacturer](/wiki/Automotive_industry "Automotive industry") | [Porsche](/wiki/Porsche "Porsche") |
| Production | 1963–present |
| Layout | |
| [Configuration](/wiki/Engine_configuration "Engine configuration") | [Flat-6](/wiki/Flat-six_engine "Flat-six engine") |
| [Displacement](/wiki/Engine_displacement "Engine displacement") | 2.0–4.2 [L](/wiki/Litre "Litre") (122–256 [cu in](/wiki/Cubic_inch "Cubic inch"))[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| [Cylinder bore](/wiki/Bore_(engine) "Bore (engine)") | 80–102.7 [mm](/wiki/Millimetre "Millimetre") (3.1–4.0 [in](/wiki/Inch "Inch"))[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) |
| [Piston stroke](/wiki/Stroke_(engine) "Stroke (engine)") | 66–80.4 [mm](/wiki/Millimetre "Millimetre") (2.6–3.2 [in](/wiki/Inch "Inch"))[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) |
| [Valvetrain](/wiki/Valvetrain "Valvetrain") | 12-valve or 24-valve, [SOHC](/wiki/SOHC "SOHC")/[DOHC](/wiki/DOHC "DOHC"), two-valves per cylinder or four-valves per cylinder[[6]](#cite_note-caranddriver.com-6) |
| Combustion | |
| [Turbocharger](/wiki/Turbocharger "Turbocharger") | Yes (some models) |
| [Fuel](/wiki/Fuel "Fuel") system | [Mechanical fuel injection](/wiki/Mechanical_fuel_injection "Mechanical fuel injection")  [Carburetor](/wiki/Carburetor "Carburetor")  [Direct fuel injection](/wiki/Gasoline_direct_injection "Gasoline direct injection") |
| Fuel type | Gasoline |
| [Oil system](/wiki/Motor_oil "Motor oil") | [Dry sump](/wiki/Dry_sump "Dry sump") |
| [Cooling system](/wiki/Internal_combustion_engine_cooling "Internal combustion engine cooling") | [Air-cooled](/wiki/Air-cooled_engine "Air-cooled engine")[[7]](#cite_note-7)  [Water-cooled](/wiki/Water-cooling "Water-cooling") |
| Output | |
| [Power output](/wiki/Power_(physics) "Power (physics)") | 110–850 [hp](/wiki/Horsepower "Horsepower") (82–634 [kW](/wiki/Kilowatt "Kilowatt")) |
| [Torque output](/wiki/Torque "Torque") | 119–730 [lb⋅ft](/wiki/Pound-foot_(torque) "Pound-foot (torque)") (161–990 [N⋅m](/wiki/Newton-metre "Newton-metre")) |
| Dimensions | |
| [Dry weight](/wiki/Vehicle_weight#Dry_weight "Vehicle weight") | 387–507 [lb](/wiki/Pound_(mass) "Pound (mass)") (176–230 [kg](/wiki/Kilogram "Kilogram")) |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3d/Porsche_mg_2167.jpg/250px-Porsche_mg_2167.jpg)](/wiki/File:Porsche_mg_2167.jpg)

Porsche flat-6 engine

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7a/Moteurs_%C3%A0_Plat_-_V_%C3%A0_180%C2%B0_vs._Boxer.png/250px-Moteurs_%C3%A0_Plat_-_V_%C3%A0_180%C2%B0_vs._Boxer.png)](/wiki/File:Moteurs_%C3%A0_Plat_-_V_%C3%A0_180%C2%B0_vs._Boxer.png)

Porsche F6 use the boxer layout (right side), not the V-180° (left)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8e/Number_01_G-series_9eleven_flat6engine_%286268827622%29.jpg/250px-Number_01_G-series_9eleven_flat6engine_%286268827622%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Number_01_G-series_9eleven_flat6engine_(6268827622).jpg)

Flat-6 engine in an older air-cooled 911

The **Porsche flat-six engine** series is a line of mechanically similar [flat-six](/wiki/Flat-six_engine "Flat-six engine") [boxer engines](/wiki/Boxer_engine "Boxer engine"), produced by [Porsche](/wiki/Porsche "Porsche") for over 60 consecutive years, since introduced in the 1963[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) [rear-engine](/wiki/Rear-engine,_rear-wheel-drive_layout "Rear-engine, rear-wheel-drive layout") [Porsche 911](/wiki/Porsche_911_(classic) "Porsche 911 (classic)"), and as [mid-rear mounted](/wiki/Rear_mid-engine,_rear-wheel-drive_layout "Rear mid-engine, rear-wheel-drive layout") racing engine in the 1966 *[Porsche 906](/wiki/Porsche_906 "Porsche 906") Carrera 6*, each with 2000cc.

All Porsche F6 boxer engines, [naturally aspirated](/wiki/Naturally_aspirated "Naturally aspirated") or [turbocharged](/wiki/Turbocharged "Turbocharged"), remained [air-cooled](/wiki/Air-cooled_engine "Air-cooled engine") with additional [oil cooling](/wiki/Oil_cooling "Oil cooling") until in the late 1970s some turbo-charged endurance racing engines were constructed partly or completely [water-cooled](/wiki/Water_cooling_(engines) "Water cooling (engines)") to be mainly used in the [Porsche 956](/wiki/Porsche_956 "Porsche 956")/962C sportscar racers in the 1980s. Related designs are still in use in the high-performance GT3/GT2 range. The regular 911 models used the air-cooled F6 until the 1990s [Porsche 993](/wiki/Porsche_993 "Porsche 993"), and starting with the [Porsche 996](/wiki/Porsche_996 "Porsche 996") are fitted with a simpler and cheaper range of water-cooled engines less suited for racing.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

The F6 engine is an evolution of the [air-cooled](/wiki/Air-cooled_engine "Air-cooled engine") [flat-four](/wiki/Flat-four_engine "Flat-four engine") [Boxer engine](/wiki/Boxer_engine "Boxer engine") designs by [Ferdinand Porsche](/wiki/Ferdinand_Porsche "Ferdinand Porsche") used in the original 1930 [Volkswagen Beetle](/wiki/Volkswagen_Beetle "Volkswagen Beetle")[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16) and continued by Porsche in the [Porsche 356](/wiki/Porsche_356 "Porsche 356") into the 1960s.

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/87/Gnome-mime-sound-openclipart.svg/60px-Gnome-mime-sound-openclipart.svg.png)

[1967 Porsche 911R](/wiki/File:Porsche_911R_(1967).ogg "File:Porsche 911R (1967).ogg")

---

[2006 Porsche Cayman S](/wiki/File:2006_Porsche_Cayman_S_engine_sound.wav "File:2006 Porsche Cayman S engine sound.wav")

---

*Problems playing these files? See [media help](/wiki/Help:Media "Help:Media").*

The flat-six engine is most often associated with their [911 model](/wiki/Porsche_911_(classic) "Porsche 911 (classic)"), Porsche's flagship rear-engined sports car which has used flat-six engines exclusively since 1963,[[17]](#cite_note-17) as the 911-bodies sold with the outgoing 356 F4 engine were called [Porsche 912](/wiki/Porsche_912 "Porsche 912").

In April 2011, Porsche announced the third generation of the 997 GT3 RS with an enlarged 4.0-litre engine having a power output of 500 PS (368 kW; 493 hp). The [naturally-aspirated](/wiki/Naturally-aspirated "Naturally-aspirated") 4.0-litre flat-six engine (the largest engine displacement offered in a street-legal 911) was introduced with their 2011 [911 (997) GT3 RS 4.0](/wiki/Porsche_911_GT3#997.2_GT3_RS_4.0 "Porsche 911 GT3"), in 2011.[[18]](#cite_note-press.porsche.com-18) The engine itself uses the crankshaft from the RSR with increased stroke dimensions (from 76.4 mm to 80.4 mm). This change increased the power output to 500 PS (368 kW; 493 hp) at 8,250 rpm and 460 N⋅m (339 lbf⋅ft) of torque at 5,750 rpm.[[19]](#cite_note-19) giving it a [power-to-weight ratio](/wiki/Power-to-weight_ratio "Power-to-weight ratio") of 365 hp per ton. Only 600 cars were built.[[18]](#cite_note-press.porsche.com-18)[[20]](#cite_note-20) At 493 hp (368 kW),[[21]](#cite_note-21) the engine is one of the most powerful six-cylinder naturally aspirated engines in any production car with a 123.25 hp (92 kW) per litre output.[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[6]](#cite_note-caranddriver.com-6)[[23]](#cite_note-23)[[24]](#cite_note-24)[[25]](#cite_note-25)

Other Porsche models that use flat-six engines are the 1970–1972 [Porsche 914/6](/wiki/Porsche_914 "Porsche 914") ([mid-engine](/wiki/Mid-engine "Mid-engine")), the 1986–1993 [Porsche 959](/wiki/Porsche_959 "Porsche 959") ([rear-engine](/wiki/Rear_engine "Rear engine")), and the 1996–2021 [Porsche Boxster/Cayman](/wiki/Porsche_Boxster/Cayman "Porsche Boxster/Cayman") (mid-engine).[[26]](#cite_note-26)[[27]](#cite_note-27)[[28]](#cite_note-28)

The [Porsche 962](/wiki/Porsche_962 "Porsche 962") [sports prototype](/wiki/Sports_prototype "Sports prototype") also used a [twin-turbocharged](/wiki/Twin-turbocharged "Twin-turbocharged") flat-six engine.[[29]](#cite_note-29)[[30]](#cite_note-30)[[31]](#cite_note-31)[[32]](#cite_note-32)

## Applications

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Porsche_flat-six_engine&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Applications")]

### Road cars

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Porsche_flat-six_engine&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Road cars")]

#### Porsche

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Porsche_flat-six_engine&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Porsche")]

* 1963–present [Porsche 911](/wiki/Porsche_911 "Porsche 911")
  + 1963–1989 original [Porsche 911](/wiki/Porsche_911_(classic) "Porsche 911 (classic)")
  + 1989–1993 [Porsche 964](/wiki/Porsche_911_(964) "Porsche 911 (964)")
  + 1994–1998 [Porsche 993](/wiki/Porsche_911_(993) "Porsche 911 (993)")
  + 1997–2006 [Porsche 996](/wiki/Porsche_911_(996) "Porsche 911 (996)")
  + 2004–2013 [Porsche 997](/wiki/Porsche_911_(997) "Porsche 911 (997)")
  + 2011–2019 [Porsche 991](/wiki/Porsche_911_(991) "Porsche 911 (991)")
  + 2019–present [Porsche 992](/wiki/Porsche_911_(992) "Porsche 911 (992)")
* 1970–1972 [Porsche 914/6](/wiki/Porsche_914 "Porsche 914")[[33]](#cite_note-33)
* 1986–1993 [Porsche 959](/wiki/Porsche_959 "Porsche 959")
* 1996–present [Porsche Boxster](/wiki/Porsche_Boxster "Porsche Boxster")[[34]](#cite_note-34)
* 2005–present [Porsche Cayman](/wiki/Porsche_Cayman "Porsche Cayman")[[35]](#cite_note-35)

#### Others

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Porsche_flat-six_engine&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Others")]

* 2014–2018 [W Motors Lykan HyperSport](/wiki/W_Motors_Lykan_HyperSport "W Motors Lykan HyperSport")
* 2019–present [W Motors Fenyr SuperSport](/wiki/W_Motors_Fenyr_SuperSport "W Motors Fenyr SuperSport")

### Race cars

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Porsche_flat-six_engine&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Race cars")]

* [Porsche 914/6 GT](/wiki/Porsche_914-6_GT "Porsche 914-6 GT")
* [Porsche 934](/wiki/Porsche_934 "Porsche 934")[[36]](#cite_note-36)[[37]](#cite_note-37)
* [Porsche 934/5](/wiki/Porsche_934/5 "Porsche 934/5")[[38]](#cite_note-38)
* [Porsche 935](/wiki/Porsche_935 "Porsche 935")[[39]](#cite_note-39)[[40]](#cite_note-40)[[41]](#cite_note-41)[[42]](#cite_note-42)[[43]](#cite_note-43)[[44]](#cite_note-44)
* [Porsche 936](/wiki/Porsche_936 "Porsche 936")
* [Porsche 956](/wiki/Porsche_956 "Porsche 956")

* [Porsche 962](/wiki/Porsche_962 "Porsche 962")
* [Porsche WSC-95](/wiki/Porsche_WSC-95 "Porsche WSC-95")
* [Porsche 911 GT1](/wiki/Porsche_911_GT1 "Porsche 911 GT1")
* [Porsche 911 GT2](/wiki/Porsche_911_GT2 "Porsche 911 GT2")
* [Porsche 911 GT3](/wiki/Porsche_911_GT3 "Porsche 911 GT3")[[45]](#cite_note-45)
* [Porsche 911 RSR](/wiki/Porsche_911_RSR "Porsche 911 RSR")
